2. EXPECTED OUTCOME: Concise summary of vterm/eat/eshell related configuration, current backend choices, and any local settings that would affect performance, mouse support, or terminal behavior.
3. REQUIRED TOOLS: read, grep, glob.
4. MUST DO: Focus on .doom.d config files and relevant emacs subtree files. Highlight whether the user already prefers a terminal backend for AI/code tools.
5. MUST NOT DO: Do not edit files. Do not inspect unrelated non-Emacs config.
6. CONTEXT: User asks how to run OpenCode so it works nicely in vterm, complaining that vterm feels slow vs kitty and mouse support is poor.

208: #+BEGIN_SRC emacs-lisp
209: (defun duplicate-line-or-region (&optional n)
210:   "Duplicate current line, or region if active.
211:     With argument N, make N copies.
212:     With negative N, comment out original line and use the absolute value."
213:   (interactive "*p")
214:   (let ((use-region (use-region-p)))
215:     (save-excursion
216:       (let ((text (if use-region
217:                       (buffer-substring (region-beginning) (region-end))
218:                     (prog1 (thing-at-point 'line)
219:                       (end-of-line)
220:                       (if (< 0 (forward-line 1)) ;Go to beginning of next line, or make a new one
221:                           (newline))))))
222:         (dotimes (i (abs (or n 1)))     ;Insert N times, or once if not specified
223:           (insert text))))
224:     (if use-region nil                  ;Only if we're working with a line (not a region)
225:       (let ((pos (- (point) (line-beginning-position)))) ;Save column
226:         (if (> 0 n)                             ;Comment out original with negative arg
227:             (comment-region (line-beginning-position) (line-end-position)))
228:         (forward-line 1)
229:         (forward-char pos)))))

5: 
6: ;; Ensure native-comp can link Homebrew GCC runtime libraries on macOS.
7: (when (eq system-type 'darwin)
8:   (let* ((gcc-exe (or (executable-find "gcc-15")
9:                       (executable-find "gcc-14")))
10:          (libemutls
11:           (when gcc-exe
